page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage options.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and vector processing are aided by hardware-level support for AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and AVX instructions.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and standard memory-to-core ratio offer a balanced profile for general-purpose workloads. It is well-suited for web serving, application hosting, Redis caching, and moderate database operations. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it less suitable for high-performance storage-heavy databases or deep learning training, but highly efficient for standard enterprise applications and microservices.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 32 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Conversely, multi-threaded decompression tasks represent a performance tradeoff, scoring in the lower quartile. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads, offering a standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It is suited for general-purpose applications, including static web serving, Redis deployments, and standard enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-8 is a general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-8 features 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core CPU performance for general workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and strong single-core performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose computing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimal for static web serving, Redis caching, and application hosting
